title: Find the Hidden Poles — Pole Identification Tool
description: An interactive 4-phase reasoning tool for Grade 6 Science – Chapter 4: Exploring Magnets. Students use a marked magnet (N and S labelled) to identify the poles of an unmarked mystery magnet, first testing with the N-pole then confirming with the S-pole.

additionalProps.completionMessage: You identified the poles using the attraction-repulsion rule — just like a real scientist! 🔬

instructions_for_student: Drag the North pole of the marked magnet near each end of the unmarked magnet. Use attraction and repulsion to figure out which end is N and which is S. Then confirm with the South pole!
subject: Science
activity_reference: Question 6 – Identifying poles of an unmarked magnet using a known magnet (Textbook p.75)

unmarkedLeftPole.description: The actual pole on the LEFT end of the mystery/unmarked magnet. The right end is automatically the opposite pole.

completionMessage.description: Message shown on the completion screen after the student finishes all phases successfully.
completionMessage.default: You identified the poles using the attraction-repulsion rule — just like a real scientist!

themeColor.description: Primary accent color (hex). Used for active step highlights and glow pulses.

_phase_flow.description: The tool has 4 sequential phases that the student progresses through

phases.title: Meet Your Magnets
phases.description: Student sees the mystery magnet (both ends ?) and the marked magnet (N=red, S=blue). Reads the golden rule of magnets. Clicks 'Start Experiment'.

phases.title: Test with N-Pole
phases.description: Student clicks to bring the N-pole near each end. Sees repulsion (red arrows) or attraction (green arrows). Infers the pole of each end. Labels each end with N or S via toggle buttons. Moves on only when labels are correct.

phases.title: Confirm with S-Pole
phases.description: Student now brings the S-pole near each labelled end to verify. S repels S → confirms S. S attracts N → confirms N. Each confirmation shows an explanation sentence.

phases.title: Done!
phases.description: Completion screen shows the fully labelled magnet, 3 gold stars, science summary, and Try Again button. Confetti rains down.

N_pole_approaching.unmarked_N_end: repulsion → student infers that end is N
N_pole_approaching.unmarked_S_end: attraction → student infers that end is S

_agent_examples.description: Default — mystery magnet's left end is N, right end is S

additionalProps.completionMessage: You identified the poles using the attraction-repulsion rule — just like a real scientist! 🔬

_agent_examples.description: Variant — mystery magnet's left end is S, right end is N

additionalProps.completionMessage: Brilliant! The left end was S and the right was N. You used repulsion and attraction like a pro!
Well done! You used repulsion and attraction to identify both poles correctly!

use_this_tool_when: Student asks about NCERT Ch4 Q6 — finding poles of an unmarked magnet using a known magnet
Question involves using attraction/repulsion to identify N and S poles
Student needs to understand why repulsion confirms same-pole and attraction confirms different-pole
Interactive experiment showing pole identification step by step
do_not_use_when: Question is about U-clip distribution on bar magnet (use magnet_selection_tool)
Question is about identifying magnets vs iron bars (use magnet_reasoning_tool)
Question is about compass direction or north-south alignment
